invariants.statement: In the online-softmax accumulator, the running row-max m_i must be finite before any exp(x - m_i) evaluation; if m_i == -inf while any x is finite, exp(x - m_i) overflows to +inf and poisons the denominator l_i for the remainder of the tile sweep.

Under fp8 (E4M3) K/V tiles, the rescale step l_new = l_prev * exp(m_prev - m_new) + row_sum_new must not underflow l_prev to zero when exp(m_prev - m_new) is within 2 ULP of the E4M3 subnormal boundary (2^-9 ~ 1.95e-3). Underflow here silently discards the prior tile's probability mass and biases the final softmax toward the current tile.
The final normalization o_i /= l_i requires l_i > 0; a zero denominator -- reachable when every tile contributes subnormal-flushed partials (see INV-002) -- yields inf/NaN in the output tensor.
